title: Verilator Internal Error with 4-state Variable During Simulation Using -fno-expand Option
body:
Hello! Recently, I've been using Verilator to simulate a Verilog file optimized by Yosys, and I inadvertently encountered the following issue.

Here is the optimized code file, which contains several thousand lines of simple Verilog statements. I tried minimizing it, but the results were not ideal.

[[opt.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19969601/opt.txt)]

This is the corresponding tb file.

[[tb.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19969675/tb.txt)]

You can reproduce the error using the following command:

```
verilator --binary -Wno-lint --timing -fno-expand opt.txt tb.txt
```

The Verilator output is as follows:

```
%Error: Internal Error: opt.v:2115:24: ../V3Number.cpp:975: toUQuad with 4-state 32'bxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx0
 2115 |   assign out51 = _0539_[0];
      |                        ^
                        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html for more assistance.
```

Although I couldn't minimize the issue, I found some interesting observations during debugging. I discovered that I need to compile both `tb.v` and `test.v` together and enable the `-fno-expand` option to trigger this error; otherwise, it can compile normally.

I'm not sure if this behavior is expected. Since the file contains several thousand lines of code, it may be difficult for developers to pinpoint and fix the issue immediately. However, I believe it would still be valuable to share this information and make others aware of the problem. :)

System details:
* Verilator version: 5.035 devel rev UNKNOWN.REV  
* OS: Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS (Jammy)  
* Kernel/Release: 22.04
code_blocks:
verilator --binary -Wno-lint --timing -fno-expand opt.txt tb.txt
%Error: Internal Error: opt.v:2115:24: ../V3Number.cpp:975: toUQuad with 4-state 32'bxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx0
 2115 |   assign out51 = _0539_[0];
      |                        ^
                        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html for more assistance.
